| SC-01 | Must have (wave1) | Automated (System Req) | **What (requirement):**  Rate Card Entitlements should NOT have Contract Lines **(???)** \- **decision to be made by Renewals** \- CLIs not a requirement for Order Mgmt ‘Rate Card Entitlement’ Contract Line Item Renewal Quantity should be 0 CLI Quantity \= 99,999 to match Entitlement Quantity Start/End Dates \= matches the main PayGo entitlement **Why (benefits):**  **Who (affected users):**     **As-is (current-state):**  **To-Be (desired state):**  **Functional Requirements:**  **Conditions (criteria to apply req):**  **Exceptions (criteria to not apply req:** **Assumptions:**  **Dependencies:**  **Acceptance Criteria:**  **Written by:** |  |  |
| SC-02 | Must have (wave1) | Automated (System Req) | **What (requirement):**  **$0 PayGo Non-Commit SKUs *should be marked as Renewable* Why (benefits):** To ensure accurate Renewals **Who (affected users):**    Renewals team **As-is (current-state):**  **To-Be (desired state):**  **Functional Requirements:**  **Conditions (criteria to apply req):**  **Exceptions (criteria to not apply req:** **Assumptions:**  **Dependencies:**  **Acceptance Criteria:** $0 Non-Commit PayGo CLIs are marked as ‘renewable’ **Written by:** David Moldvai |  |  |
| SC-03 | Must have (wave1) | Automated (System Req) | **What (requirement):**  CLIs are required only for the PayGo SKUs (not the Rate Card Products)  **Why (benefits):** To ensure accurate Renewals **Who (affected users):**    Renewals team **As-is (current-state):**  **To-Be (desired state):**  **Functional Requirements:**  **Conditions (criteria to apply req):**  **Exceptions (criteria to not apply req:** **Assumptions:**  **Dependencies:**  **Acceptance Criteria:** $0 Non-Commit PayGo CLIs are marked as ‘renewable’ **Written by:** David Moldvai |  |  |

13. ### **Entitlements (David)**

| \# | Priority | Delivery Mode | Requirement Description | Example/ Mock-up Link | Remarks/Qs |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| ENT-01 | Must have (wave1) | Automated (System Req) | **What (requirement):**  All **Entitlements** on the PayGo order must be **automatically created** (PayGo \+ Rate Card products) **Why (benefits):** Reduce manual effort and minimize mistakes of Installed Base having to manually create Rate Card entitlements. Rate Card products  **Who (affected users):**    Installed Base / End User **As-is (current-state):** Entitlements have to be manually created by IB **To-Be (desired state):** System to automatically create the Rate Card Entitlements **Functional Requirements:**  **Conditions (criteria to apply req):**  **Exceptions (criteria to not apply req:** **Assumptions:**  **Dependencies:**  **Acceptance Criteria:**  **Written by:** David Moldvai |  | Follow up on decision that Rate Card products will not require Entitlements |
| ENT-02 | Must have (wave1) | Automated (System Req) | **What (requirement):**  **MSP PayGo Rate Card Entitlements** must be **identifiable** on the **Entitlement** **object** ; and *easily* differentiable from standard Phone Support Entitlements **Why (benefits):** Installed Base / Topline teams need to know that these do not impact ARR **Who (affected users):**    Installed Base / Topline **As-is (current-state):**  **To-Be (desired state):** Rate Card Entitlements should be easily identifiable for ARR reporting purposes **Functional Requirements:**  **Conditions (criteria to apply req):**  **Exceptions (criteria to not apply req:** **Assumptions:**  **Dependencies:** This field should also be used to identify Enterprise Flex Rate Card Entitlements vs MSP PayGo Rate Card Entitlements **Acceptance Criteria:**  **Written by:** David Moldvai  |  |  |
| ENT-03 | Must have (wave1) | Automated (System Req) | **What (requirement):**  **Rate Card Entitlements** must be created automatically with the following info: Entitlement Name Product Type (*IT can suggest how to identify these*) Subtype (*IT can suggest how to identify these*) Account Name Start / End Dates Quantity \= 99,999 Order Number Order Quantity Renewal Quantity \= 0 Service Contract(?) \- required for Rate Card Ents? Contract Line Item(?) \- required for Rate Card Ents? \- awaiting Ramya’s answer ***Identifier \= PayGo Rate Card (IT can determine how best to identify these)*** **Is *Financial Information* required by any teams on the Rate Card Product? David to check in with Suhas/Shubham**  **Why (benefits):**  **Who (affected users):**     **As-is (current-state):** All of this info is manually inputted for Crawl phase PayGo **To-Be (desired state):** Automated information populated **Functional Requirements:**  **Conditions (criteria to apply req):**  **Exceptions (criteria to not apply req:** **Assumptions:**  **Dependencies:** This field should also be used to identify Enterprise Flex Rate Card Entitlements vs MSP PayGo Rate Card Entitlements **Acceptance Criteria:**  **Written by:** David Moldvai  |  |  |
| ENT-04 | Nice to have | Automated (System Req) | **What (requirement):**  Single wrapper Entitlement for PayGo main SKU (similar to RSVX with Utility) that covers the whole term **Why (benefits):**  **Who (affected users):**     **As-is (current-state):** All of this info is manually inputted for Crawl phase PayGo **To-Be (desired state):** Automated information populated **Functional Requirements:**  **Conditions (criteria to apply req):**  **Exceptions (criteria to not apply req:** **Assumptions:**  **Dependencies:** This field should also be used to identify Enterprise Flex Rate Card Entitlements vs MSP PayGo Rate Card Entitlements **Acceptance Criteria:**  **Written by:** David Moldvai |  |  |
| ENT-05 | Must have (wave1) | Automated (System Req) | **What (requirement):**  When new rate card products are released and added to the MSP PayGo Rate Card object, this requires the: New Rate Card Product to be provisioned to ACTIVE, existing PayGo customers  Entitlement to be automatically created with the accurate details for all ACTIVE existing PayGo customers The Support End Date of the newly released Rate Card product’s Entitlement should co-term to the Existing Rate Card products’ Entitlements’ End Date The Support Start Date of the newly released Rate Card product is the day it was *provided* to the customer **Why (benefits):**  **Who (affected users):**     **As-is (current-state):** All of this info is manually inputted for Crawl phase PayGo **To-Be (desired state):** Automated information populated **Functional Requirements:**  **Conditions (criteria to apply req):**  **Exceptions (criteria to not apply req:** **Assumptions:**  **Dependencies:** This field should also be used to identify Enterprise Flex Rate Card Entitlements vs MSP PayGo Rate Card Entitlements **Acceptance Criteria:**  **Written by:** David Moldvai |  |  |

14. ### **Invoice (Sharon)**

| ID | Priority | Delivery Mode | Requirement Description | Example/ Mock-up Link | Remarks/Qs |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| INV-01 | Must have (wave1) | Automated (System Req) | **What (requirement):**  NetSuite must automatically create an accurate billing schedule upon SO fulfillment for all PayGo Commit, overage and NC orders based on a usage cutoff of 25th of each month. No manual billing schedule creation should be required. All PayGo billing lines \- initial commit/reserve, overages, and monthly usage \- must appear as ready-to-bill in the NetSuite Invoice SO screen upon fulfillment, enabling AR to batch-create invoices via the existing process. **Why (benefits):** Ensures billing is system-driven and auditable, eliminates missed or delayed invoices, and strengthens the SOX billing cycle control. **Who (affected users):**   NetSuite Admin (configuration), AR  **As-is (current-state):**  **To-Be (desired state):** Every PayGo Commit SO fulfillment event triggers automatic creation of a complete, accurate billing schedule in NetSuite with no manual steps. **Functional Requirements:**  FR-01: System auto-generates a billing schedule on SO fulfillment for all PayGo Commit SOs. FR-02: Billing trigger is SO fulfillment \- no manual initiation by any team. FR-03: Billing schedule data is retained in NetSuite and traceable to the fulfillment event for SOX audit. **Conditions (criteria to apply req):**  SO is classified as PayGo Commit, overage for PayGo Commit, or PayGo NC in NetSuite. SO has reached fulfilled status. **Exceptions (criteria to not apply req:** **Assumptions:**  **Dependencies:**  Accurate creation of PayGo Commit orders, PayGo Commit overage orders based on usage through the 25th of each month, and PayGo NC orders based on usage through the 25th of each month. Consistent PayGo Commit order type classification on the SO. **Acceptance Criteria:** 100% of PayGo Commit SOs in UAT auto-generate a billing schedule upon fulfillment with zero manual steps required. **Written by: [Sharon Zhang](mailto:sharon.zhang@rubrik.com)** |  |  |
| INV-02 | Must have (wave1) | Automated (System Req) | **What (requirement):**  Consolidation of Overage and Reserve Invoices \- Where billing period and SO/customer attributes align, overage and reserve invoice lines must be consolidated into a single invoice with minimal manual intervention. The system automatically sends out the consolidated invoices at scheduled times.  **Why (benefits):** Reduces invoice volume sent to customers, lowers AR processing overhead, and simplifies the customer payment experience. **Who (affected users):**   Revenue IT (currently manually consolidate the invoices), AR (manually send out the consolidated invoices) **As-is (current-state):** Overage and reserve invoices are generated separately with no systematic consolidation \- resulting in higher invoice volume and increased AR effort. The consolidated invoices are manually sent out, creating AR overhead. **To-Be (desired state):** Where consolidation criteria are met, overage and reserve lines are combined into one invoice automatically or via a minimal, documented manual step. System automatically sends the consolidated invoices. **Functional Requirements:**  FR-04: System consolidates overage and reserve lines into a single invoice where billing period, customer entity, currency, and payment terms match. FR-05: Any manual steps required for consolidation are documented, limited to exception handling, and do not require AR to build invoices from scratch FR-06: The system automatically includes consolidated invoices in the scheduled invoice send batch. **Conditions (criteria to apply req):**  Overage and reserve lines share the same billing period. Lines belong to the same PayGo Commit deal. **Exceptions (criteria to not apply req:** **Assumptions:**  **Dependencies:**  **Acceptance Criteria:**  In UAT, overage \+ reserve lines meeting consolidation criteria produce a single invoice. The system automatically picks up the consolidated invoices in the scheduled invoice send batch. **Written by: [Sharon Zhang](mailto:sharon.zhang@rubrik.com)** |  |  |
| INV-03 | Must have (wave1) | Automated (System Req) | **What (requirement):**  All overage and monthly usage invoices must include itemized evidence of usage: product name, usage quantity, unit of measure (UOM), unit price, and total price per line. **Why (benefits):** Provides customers the detail required to approve and pay invoices promptly. Reduces disputes and supports auditability of variable usage billings. **Who (affected users):**    Initiator (not sure which team) to ensure the right system input, System admin for process configuration, Customer, AR **As-is (current-state):** Overage not expected, no system solution **To-Be (desired state):** Usage evidence fields are auto-populated on every overage and monthly usage invoice from a system-of-record source. AR does not manually enter or compile usage data. **Functional Requirements:**  FR-7: All overage and monthly usage invoices include line-level fields: product name, usage quantity, UOM, unit price, and total price. FR-8: Usage data is sourced from the system-of-record (usage reporting system or approved data feed) \- not manually entered by any team. FR-9: The data population process is controlled and auditable. **Conditions (criteria to apply req):**  Invoice is for an overage or monthly usage order type. Usage data for the billing period (through 25th of each month) is available and finalized in the source system. **Exceptions (criteria to not apply req:** Periods where usage data has not yet been finalized or requires manual review, billing is deferred until data is finalized/signed off by channel ops. **Assumptions:**  A system-of-record for usage data (product breakdown, quantity, UOM, pricing) exists and can feed into NetSuite. Usage data is finalized and available before the invoice generation date for the relevant billing period. **Dependencies:**  Integration or data feed between the usage reporting system and NetSuite. Defined data schema for usage evidence fields agreed upon by IT and Product/Engineering. **Acceptance Criteria:**  All overage and monthly usage invoices in UAT contain populated fields for: product name, quantity, UOM, unit price, and total price. Fields are sourced from the system-of-record with no manual data entry required. Channel ops confirms data accuracy against source usage reports. **Written by: [Sharon Zhang](mailto:sharon.zhang@rubrik.com)** |  |  |

15. ### **Revenue Recognition (Sharon)**

| ID | Priority | Delivery Mode | Requirement Description | Example/ Mock-up Link | Remarks/Qs |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| REV-01 | Must have (wave1) | Automated (System Req) | **What (requirement):**  When new products are added to an existing rate card as part of a contract amendment, there must be evidence that the products are provisioned correctly with the appropriate rates for the MSP/customer. **Why (benefits):** This is a revrec requirement for contract amendment **Who (affected users):**  Provisioning team, systems, revenue team   **As-is (current-state):** No standardized process **To-Be (desired state):** New products are provisioned for the MSP after the required contract amendment configuration, enabling the MSP to access the products and allowing the system to calculate usage for overage and/or usage-based billing. **Functional Requirements:** to be defined by the process owner (provisioning team?) **Conditions (criteria to apply req):**  **Exceptions (criteria to not apply req:** **Assumptions:**  **Dependencies:**  **Acceptance Criteria:** There is one consistent source of evidence that the products are provisioned correctly with the appropriate rates for the MSP/customer. **Written by: Sharon Zhang** |  |  |

21. ### **Disti Ops (Amy)**

| ID | Priority | Delivery Mode | Requirement Description | Example/ Mock-up Link | Remarks/Qs |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Disti-01 | Must have (wave1) | Automated (System Req) | **What (requirement):** Invoice needs to follow the current scale utility layout clearly showing the original distributor PO\# as well as commit vs on demand/overage breakdown.  **Why (benefits):** The distributor needs to be able to cross reference the PO and order on their side and let the MSP know the breakdown amounts on the invoice given the SKU is the same. **Who (affected users):** Accounts Receivable and Distributors    **As-is (current-state):** Mirror the scale utility layout **To-Be (desired state):** Mirror the scale utility layout **Functional Requirements:** If in the commit model there is an overage then there should be a consolidated invoice for the order showing the commit lines vs overage lines. Any Overages should include details as to the exact products used, their bill rates, the total usage per product, unit of measure to supplement the total overage bill. **Conditions (criteria to apply req):** Applies to all PayGo orders (commit and no commit) **Exceptions (criteria to not apply req:** none **Assumptions:** all order types connected to PayGo  **Dependencies:** order is PayGo **Acceptance Criteria:** Invoice shows the original distributor PO\# as well as commit vs on demand/overage breakdown.  **Written by:** Amy Mead | [https://lucid.app/lucidchart/462cde37-55ab-4943-ba6d-25844b2881b4/edit?invitationId=inv\_a820f9f5-ec23-4f3c-abce-340e56aa40fe\&page=qDjnNe34sBAL\#](https://lucid.app/lucidchart/462cde37-55ab-4943-ba6d-25844b2881b4/edit?invitationId=inv_a820f9f5-ec23-4f3c-abce-340e56aa40fe&page=qDjnNe34sBAL#) |  |
| Disti-02 | Must have (wave1) | Automated (System Req) | **What (requirement):** Rebates should apply to all PayGo orders whether they are commit or no commit and whether they are new, addendums, overages or renewals **Why (benefits):** The rebate is needed for distributors to be able to process PayGo orders **Who (affected users):** OM, revenue, distributors   **As-is (current-state):** Same rebate categories and order types as scale utility that currently trigger rebates \- if any new categories are created those must also be set up for rebates with current system logic **To-Be (desired state):** Rebates applies to all PayGo orders whether they are commit or no commit and whether they are new, addendums, overages or renewals **Functional Requirements:** Rebate needs to be applied automatically to the order **Conditions (criteria to apply req):** Any PayGo order **Exceptions (criteria to not apply req:** none **Assumptions:** all order types connected to PayGo will trigger a rebate to the distributor **Dependencies:** order is PayGo **Acceptance Criteria:** Rebate is automatically applied so it’s sent to the distributor monthly **Written by:** Amy Mead |  |  |
| Disti-03 | Nice to have | Automated (System Req) | **What (requirement):**  Provide a usage report to distributors and partners to help back any overage billing invoices that Rubrik initiates. It is important to have a clear, granular allocation in a readable format (e.g., .csv or .xlsx) **Why (benefits):** The partner can proactively review and object to any data that does not look correct reducing the amount of potential debooks due to incorrect data **Who (affected users):** MSP Ops, Distributors, MSP partners    **As-is (current-state):** Currently nothing exists **To-Be (desired state):** Report would automatically be sent to distributors and MSP partners prior to invoicing **Functional Requirements:**  **Conditions (criteria to apply req):** Any PayGo order **Exceptions (criteria to not apply req:** none **Assumptions:**  **Dependencies:** order is PayGo **Acceptance Criteria:** Reporting automatically sent \- may need to check with [Toni Hogan](mailto:toni.hogan@rubrik.com)on what should be included to back up any overage charges **Written by:** Amy Mead |  |  |
| Disti-04 | Must have (wave1) | Automated (System Req) | **What (requirement):** Quote PDF needs to include commitment, billing schedule, ramp schedule, rate card and any legal terms to be passed down (including verbiage about the commit and overage being billed using the same SKU). Any QTY must be 1 or greater (systems can’t handle 0\) **Why (benefits):** The quote PDF is what is used for the disti to quote and pass down any needed info to the MSP partner **Who (affected users):** CPQ, MSP Ops, Distributors, Legal   **As-is (current-state):**  **To-Be (desired state):** Quote PDF needs to include commitment, billing schedule, ramp schedule, rate card and any legal terms to be passed down (including verbiage about the commit and overage being billed using the same SKU). Any QTY must be 1 or greater (systems can’t handle 0\) **Functional Requirements:**  **Conditions (criteria to apply req):** Any PayGo order **Exceptions (criteria to not apply req:** none **Assumptions:**  **Dependencies:** quote is PayGo **Acceptance Criteria:** Quote PDF includes all needed SKUs, pricing, tables and legal language  **Written by:** Amy Mead |  |  |
| Disti-05 | Must have (wave1) | Automated (System Req) | **What (requirement):** Must have a systematic way to terminate contract licenses after X months if the MSP partner has not paid the distributor in a no commit model once we are charging **Why (benefits):** Needed option to terminate for disti to sign off on doing no commit model **Who (affected users):** MSP Ops, Distributors, IT, Revenue    **As-is (current-state):**  **To-Be (desired state):**  **Functional Requirements:**  **Conditions (criteria to apply req):** Any PayGo no commit order **Exceptions (criteria to not apply req:** **Assumptions:**  **Dependencies:**  **Acceptance Criteria:**  **Written by:** Amy Mead |  |  |

# **Appendix**

## Appendix A: Current State vs Target State Provisioning Flow

There are two distinct systems involved in getting licenses into RSC. Understanding both is critical because PayGo requirements span both:

| Current State | Target State |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Current Account Provisioning Flow (Pacman):** This flow handles the one-time creation of an RSC account and enablement of products when a customer order is fulfilled. It is event-driven. `Salesforce Order (closed-won / accepted)	↓ triggersPacman Service (Python gRPC — provisioning orchestrator)	↓ parses order JSON, creates DNS entry, selects target deploymentBroker Service (routes to correct RSC deployment)	↓provision-order korg taskchain (Go — runs async)	├── CreateAccount RPC (am-service) → creates account DB, UUID, metadata	├── AddUser → creates initial owner/admin users	├── Product Enablers → enables each ordered product (O365, RCV, Radar, CloudNative, etc.)    └── addClusterOrder → sets up license/SKU records	↓Update Salesforce (fulfillment status, account URL, timestamps)`  **Current Entitlement Sync Flow:** This flow handles the ongoing synchronization of license metadata (capacity, dates, status) from Salesforce into RSC. It is polling-based. `Salesforce (Entitlement objects with SKU-level detail)	↓ nightly ETLSnowflake (V_ENTITLEMENT_FEATURE_DETAILS)	↓ every 4 hours (fetch-customer-entitlements korg job)am-service SyncEntitlements RPC	↓RSC (entitlements table per customer account)	↓License Dashboard + Dedicated Tenant wizard (uses entitlement names)`   | **Target PayGo Flow: Target Account Provisioning Flow:**  `CPQ (Rate card order with ~20 products) 	↓ Order Accepted [Account Provisioning via Pacman] Salesforce Order JSON (PayGo-formatted, compatible with existing Pacman endpoint) 	↓ automated trigger (replaces manual SPARK ticket) Pacman ProvisionOrder → DNS → provision-order taskchain 	├── CreateAccount (if new MSP) or skip (if existing) 	├── Product Enablers (each rate card product maps to an existing enabler) 	└── Update SFDC (fulfillment status, URLs)`   **Target Entitlement Sync Flow:** `Salesforce PayGo Entitlements (quantity 99,999, SKU Marketing Names, edition metadata)	↓ nightly ETLSnowflake (V_ENTITLEMENT_FEATURE_DETAILS — enhanced with PayGo attributes)	↓ automated sync (event-driven or accelerated polling)RSC (entitlements table — PayGo-aware)	↓License Dashboard (shows "PayGO") + Tenant wizard (uses SKU Marketing Names)	↓Usage collection → edition-tier attribution → monthly billing export` |
